episode_summary: This episode covers centuries-old bowhead whales that predate Moby-Dick, the European Space Agency’s extreme acoustic test facility, South Korea’s school coffee ban (and coffee’s health effects and history), and China’s chicken facial-recognition system alongside wider facial-recognition oddities.

facts.fact: My fact is that there are whales alive today who were alive before Moby Dick was written.
My fact this week is that the European Space Agency has a sound system so loud that if you heard it, it would kill you.
My fact this week is that South Korea has banned coffee in schools.
My fact this week is that facial recognition technology for chickens allows you to pre-order a specific chicken and watch it grow on the farm before it ends up on your plate.
